articles.title: TNF-α Inflammatory Signaling Suppresses Neurogenesis
articles.summary: Neurogenesis is the name given to the creation of new neurons in the central nervous system, arising from neural stem cell populations, maturing, and then merging with existing neural networks. Neurogenesis is essential to memory and to the maintenance of brain tissue, the only way to replace neurons lost to damage or dysfunction. The pace of neurogenesis declines with age and in neurodegenerative conditions. Here, researchers investigate the link between inflammatory signaling and lost neurogenesis. The aged brain, like the aged body, is characterized by continual unresolved inflammatory signaling, a maladaptive reaction to forms of cell and tissue damage that changes cell behavior for the worse. It is disruptive to tissue structure and function. Any comprehensive package of rejuvenation therapies will have to include some […]
